definition: The thesis that India will replace China as the marginal commodity demand driver lacks quantitative support. India's per-capita metal consumption is ~1/8 of China's at comparable development stages. At China's 2005 intensity, India would add only 20-30% of China's 2000s increment. India's services-led growth model implies structurally lower commodity intensity.
evidence: India per-capita metal consumption ~1/8 of China at comparable stages
At China's 2005 intensity: only 20-30% of China's 2000s increment
India is services-led at structurally lower commodity intensity
No agent mounted a credible bull case for India as demand replacement
open_questions: Could India's infrastructure deficit create a catch-up surge different from the services-led trajectory?
What would India's urbanization path imply for commodity demand under various scenarios?
